question.text: A ULIP policyholder wants to surrender the policy after 3 years of investment. What is the regulatory position on this?

answer.text: The policyholder cannot surrender before completing the 5-year lock-in period; the fund will be moved to a discontinuation fund with a maximum charge of Rs. 6,000
The policyholder can surrender at any time and will receive the full fund value minus a 2% exit load
The policyholder can surrender after 3 years with a minimum guaranteed surrender value of 30% of premiums paid
The policyholder must wait for the full policy term to complete before any withdrawal is permitted

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ULIPs have a mandatory 5-year lock-in period as per IRDAI regulations. If a policyholder discontinues before 5 years, the fund value (after deducting a discontinuation charge of maximum Rs. 6,000) is moved to a discontinuation fund. The proceeds are paid out only after the lock-in period of 5 years is completed. **The Trap:** The 30% minimum surrender value applies to traditional policies, not ULIPs. ULIPs have their own discontinuation framework. There is no "2% exit load" concept -- the charge is capped at Rs. 6,000 in absolute terms. The funds are not locked until the full policy term, only until 5 years. **Cross-Exam Context:** ULIP discontinuation rules are heavily tested in both NISM X-B and NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) exams for comparison purposes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 6,000 cap on discontinuation charges is the maximum -- actual charges vary by year of discontinuation and are typically lower in later years. Always check the policy document.
